section_map.title: FELLOW EDITION
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Version 1.0 | March 2026 For use by SOZO Fellow clinicians under Doctor supervision © 2026 SOZO Brain Center. All rights reserved. CONFIDENTIAL.

section_map.title: About This Handbook
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: This handbook provides a complete step-by-step guide to the SOZO clinical pathway for Parkinson’s Disease (PD) using evidence-based neuromodulation. It is designed for daily clinical use by SOZO Fello

section_map.title: Evidence-Based Neuromodulation Approach
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: The SOZO evidence-based approach follows a structured clinical decision pathway that matches neuromodulation protocols to patient phenotype using published research evidence:

section_map.title: Available Modalities at SOZO
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: ⚠ IMPORTANT: Off-Label Disclosure TPS (NEUROLITH®) use in Parkinson’s disease is INVESTIGATIONAL and OFF-LABEL. CE marking applies to Alzheimer’s disease only. Mandatory off-label disclosure and Docto

section_map.title: GOVERNANCE RULE
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: No Fellow or Clinical Assistant may independently modify a treatment plan, change a montage, or adjust parameters without Doctor authorisation. All protocol decisions must be approved by the supervisi

section_map.title: Purpose
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Before any diagnostic or therapeutic procedure is performed, the patient must provide written informed consent. This stage ensures the patient fully understands the nature, risks, benefits, and altern

section_map.title: 2.1 Welcome and Documentation
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Upon arrival at the clinic, the patient is welcomed by the administrative or clinical team, completes required registration documentation, and provides identification and emergency contact details.

section_map.title: 2.2 Consent Discussion
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: The treating Doctor (or designated clinician) conducts a structured consent discussion covering:

section_map.title: 2.3 Obtain Signatures
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Patient signs the Informed Consent Form. Doctor co-signs confirming the consent discussion took place. Witness signature obtained (if required by local regulation).

section_map.title: MANDATORY
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: No diagnostic or therapeutic procedures may be performed prior to informed consent. TPS for PD requires specific, documented off-label disclosure with Doctor sign-off.

section_map.title: Checklist
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: ☐ Patient registration completed ☐ Consent discussion conducted by Doctor ☐ All modalities explained including off-label TPS status ☐ Patient questions addressed ☐ Consent form signed (patient + Docto

section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Signed informed consent. Patient cleared for assessment.

section_map.title: Purpose
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: This stage provides structured phenotyping and baseline quantification. The patient is evaluated by the Fellow (or trained clinical assistant) who conducts a structured clinical interview and performs

section_map.title: 3.1 Structured Clinical Interview
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Conduct a systematic interview covering:

section_map.title: 3.2 Digitise Medical Documentation
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Scan or digitally archive all medical documents the patient has brought. Record what was provided in the Documentation Checklist section of the intake form.

section_map.title: 3.3 SOZO PRS — Patient Rating System (Baseline)
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: The SOZO PRS is the standardised psychometric tool used to quantify symptom severity across clinically relevant domains. It establishes the baseline against which all future progress will be measured.

section_map.title: KEY PRINCIPLE
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Select relevant domains based on diagnosis and presenting phenotype. Focus only on clinically meaningful domains — do not administer all scales to every patient. Motor Symptoms Domain — score each rel

section_map.title: 3.4 Preliminary Phenotype Impression
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Based on the interview and PRS results, form a preliminary clinical impression:

section_map.title: Checklist
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: ☐ Structured clinical interview completed ☐ All medical documentation digitised and archived ☐ SOZO PRS baseline scores recorded (relevant domains) ☐ Preliminary phenotype impression documented ☐ Pati

section_map.title: Output
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Baseline phenotype profile with quantified symptom severity. Data ready for medical examination (Stage 4).

section_map.title: Forms Used
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Patient Intake & Medical History (Document 03) + Psychological Intake & PRS Baseline (Document 04)

section_map.title: Purpose
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: The patient is examined by the SOZO Doctor (with Fellow assistance). This stage provides clinical validation of the phenotype identified in Stage 3, and informs the preliminary selection of neuromodul

section_map.title: 4.1 Motor Examination
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Conduct a systematic motor examination using the Clinical Examination Checklist. Score each domain using the 0–4 scale (0=Normal, 1=Slight, 2=Mild, 3=Moderate, 4=Severe):

section_map.title: 4.2 Standard Neurological Examination
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: A structured neurological examination ensures anatomical correlation and exclusion of focal structural lesions:

section_map.title: 4.3 Cognitive & Mood Screening
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Administer standardised cognitive and mood screening tools:

section_map.title: 4.4 Phenotype Identification
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Based on the combined motor examination, neurological examination, and cognitive/mood screening, the Doctor formally identifies the clinical phenotype:

section_map.title: 4.5 EEG Assessment (if available)
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: When technically available, EEG assessment provides additional neurophysiological data. The Doctor interprets EEG findings in the context of the clinical examination and phenotype identification.

section_map.title: Checklist
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: ☐ Motor examination completed (all domains scored) ☐ Neurological examination completed ☐ Cognitive screening completed (MoCA, trails, digit span) ☐ Mood screening completed (PHQ-9, GAD-7) ☐ Clinical

section_map.title: Output
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Validated clinical phenotype and preliminary NIBS strategy.

section_map.title: Forms Used
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Clinical Examination Checklist (Document 05 in the Patient Folder)

section_map.title: Purpose
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: After completion of the comprehensive assessment (Stages 3–4), the clinical team prepares a detailed clinical report and schedules the follow-up appointment for report discussion.

section_map.title: 5.1 Compile Assessment Data
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: The clinical team gathers all data from the assessment stages:

section_map.title: 5.2 Draft the Clinical Report
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: The report should include: reason for referral, clinical history summary, psychological assessment findings, SOZO PRS baseline scores, clinical examination findings (neurological exam, cognitive scree

section_map.title: 5.3 Doctor Review and Approval
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: The report must be reviewed and approved by the treating Doctor before being sent to the patient.

section_map.title: 5.4 Communicate with Patient
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Inform the patient that the comprehensive clinical report will be sent via email. Schedule a follow-up appointment for report discussion, treatment plan finalisation, and cost explanation.

section_map.title: Checklist
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: ☐ All assessment data compiled ☐ Clinical report drafted ☐ Report reviewed and approved by Doctor ☐ Report sent to patient via email ☐ Follow-up appointment scheduled

section_map.title: Output
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Written comprehensive clinical report. Follow-up appointment confirmed.

section_map.title: Forms Used
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Clinical Report Template (Document 06 in the Patient Folder)

section_map.title: Purpose
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: During the second visit, the clinical report is reviewed in detail with the patient. The treatment plan is finalised, costs are explained, and if the patient decides to proceed, a structured treatment

section_map.source_excerpt_sample: The Doctor reviews the following with the patient: identified phenotype and what it means in simple terms, proposed neuromodulation strategy and the rationale for selected modalities, why specific bra

section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Explain the complete treatment plan including:

section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Explain the SOZO sequencing logic to the patient in accessible language:

section_map.title: Purpose
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: The patient undergoes the prescribed course of neuromodulation sessions in the clinic. During this phase, patients are also trained for home-based therapy and, if indicated, devices are purchased and

section_map.source_excerpt_sample: ☐ Verify patient identity and session number ☐ Record medication timing (last levodopa dose, ON/OFF state) ☐ Check skin integrity at electrode sites ☐ Verify impedance (<15 kΩ for tDCS; do NOT stimula

section_map.title: 7.2 Deliver Treatment Session

section_map.title: 7.3 Session Documentation
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: After each session, document in the Session Log: date, time, session number; modality, montage, and parameters used; medication state (ON/OFF, timing of last dose); concurrent task performed; any adve

section_map.source_excerpt_sample: During the in-clinic phase, train the patient (and caregiver if applicable) in:

section_map.title: 7.6 Issue Home Protocol Prescription
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: When the patient transitions to home-based therapy, the Doctor issues a written prescription covering: prescribed device(s) and specific montage configuration, stimulation parameters (intensity, durat

section_map.title: ⚠ HOME USE SAFETY
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: For HDCkit tDCS, all settings are pre-programmed by the Doctor via HDCprog — the patient cannot modify parameters. Home use applies to tDCS, taVNS, and CES only. TPS is clinic-only.

section_map.title: Checklist (Per Session)
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: ☐ Pre-session checks complete (skin, impedance, medication timing) ☐ Treatment delivered per prescribed protocol ☐ Concurrent task completed ☐ Post-session skin inspection ☐ Session documented in Sess

section_map.title: Output
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Active neuromodulation delivered. Patient trained for home continuation. Session data collected for outcome evaluation.

section_map.title: Forms Used
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Session Log & AE Tracker (Document 08) + Device Training & Home Protocol (Document 09)

section_map.title: Purpose
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: After 2–3 months of treatment, the patient undergoes a full reassessment, repeating the psychological assessment, medical examination, psychometric retesting, and EEG (if applicable). This ensures obj

section_map.title: 8.1 Repeat Psychological Assessment (Stage 3)
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Conduct a structured clinical interview to assess any changes in complaints, mood, function, and medication. Re-administer the SOZO PRS across all baseline domains. Record follow-up scores alongside b

section_map.title: 8.2 Repeat Medical Examination (Stage 4)
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Repeat the neurological examination, cognitive and mood screening, and EEG (if applicable). Document all findings alongside baseline values.

section_map.title: 8.3 Compare Baseline vs. Follow-Up
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: For each domain, compare baseline and follow-up scores and assess whether changes are clinically meaningful:

section_map.title: ⚠ AVOIDING FALSE CLASSIFICATION
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Standardise medication timing between baseline and follow-up. If baseline was measured in OFF state and follow-up in ON state (or vice versa), you may create a false responder or false non-responder.

section_map.title: 8.5 Make Clinical Decision
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: ☐ CONTINUE current protocol (patient is responding) ☐ MODIFY protocol (partial response → proceed to Stage 9) ☐ FULL REASSESSMENT (non-responder → re-phenotype and revise) ☐ TRANSITION to home-only ma

section_map.title: Output
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Objective outcome comparison (baseline vs. follow-up). Response classification. Evidence-based clinical decision.

section_map.title: Forms Used
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Follow-Up Assessment (Document 10 in the Patient Folder)

section_map.title: Purpose
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Based on the follow-up findings from Stage 8, the treatment is optimised. This stage represents the iterative reassessment loop that ensures treatment remains aligned with the patient’s evolving clini

section_map.title: 9.1 Analyse Treatment Response
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Review which domains improved, which did not, and which worsened. Review adverse event history from the treatment block. Assess medication timing variability and its potential impact on results. Revie

section_map.title: 9.2 Reassess Phenotype
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: For non-responders or partial responders, formally reassess: Has the dominant phenotype changed since baseline? Are there new symptoms or symptom clusters? Does the original clinical assessment still

section_map.title: 9.3 Revise Target Selection
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Update the stimulation target based on new clinical data. Consider alternative or additional cortical targets. Review EEG findings if new data is available.

section_map.title: 9.4 Modify Protocol
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: The Doctor may make the following modifications:

section_map.title: 9.5 Non-Responder Escalation Pathway
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: If no meaningful improvement after 8–10 sessions, follow this escalation:

section_map.title: ⚠ SAFETY RULE
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: DO NOT escalate intensity beyond safety limits. The protocol requires returning to systematic reassessment rather than simple dose escalation.

section_map.title: 9.6 Issue Updated Treatment Plan
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Document all modifications with rationale. Issue updated home protocol prescription (if applicable). Set the next review date. Continue therapy for another 2–3 months under the updated protocol.

section_map.title: THE ITERATION LOOP
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Stages 7 → 8 → 9 form a continuous cycle. After each optimisation (Stage 9), the patient returns to active treatment (Stage 7) under the updated protocol, followed by reassessment (Stage 8), and furth

section_map.title: Output
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Updated treatment protocol. Revised clinical assessment. Patient continues under the optimised plan.

section_map.title: Forms Used
section_map.source_excerpt_sample: Therapy Optimisation Record (Document 11 in the Patient Folder)

sample_excerpts: Integrating tDCS (Newronika HDCkit / PlatoScience) • TPS (NEUROLITH®) • taVNS • CES
For use by SOZO Fellow clinicians under Doctor supervision
© 2026 SOZO Brain Center. All rights reserved. CONFIDENTIAL.

This handbook provides a complete step-by-step guide to the SOZO clinical pathway for Parkinson’s Disease (PD) using evidence-based neuromodulation. It is designed for daily clinical use by SOZO Fellow clinicians operating under the supervision of the treating Doctor.

Each chapter corresponds to one stage of the patient journey, from initial appointment scheduling through active treatment and long-term therapy optimisation. The handbook integrates content from the SOZO Evidence-Based NIBS Clinical Protocol and the SOZO PD Assessment documents.
